description: User message expresses disappointment — a gap between expectation and reality that has settled into resignation or sadness rather than active anger. The user had hopes that were not met. Distinct from frustrated_angry (lower arousal, more reflective) and anxious_worried (past-facing, not future-threatening). Common in product reviews, post-purchase support, churn signals. Recommended downstream action: empathy-first response, acknowledge the gap specifically, consider retention gesture (refund, discount, feature roadmap commitment), avoid defensive explanations.

en: this is not what I expected
I feel let down by this
I thought this would be better
this could have been much better
quality is not what was advertised
the result is mediocre at best
you let me down this time
this was a waste of money
I was counting on this working
this barely does what it promises
fell short of what I needed
I had high hopes but they're gone
